heading: Personnel
label: 205i Personnel
notes: References in Other Laws to GS–16, 17, or 18 Pay RatesReferences in laws to the rates of pay for GS–16, 17, or 18, or to maximum rates of pay under the General Schedule, to be considered references to rates payable under specified sections of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, see section 529 [title I, § 101(c)(1)] of , set out in a note under .Pub. L. 101–509section 5376 of Title 5

children.heading: Executive Director; appointment; tenure; duties
children.label: a Executive Director; appointment; tenure; duties
children.text: The Board shall appoint a qualified individual to serve as the Executive Director of the Board at the pleasure of the Board. The Executive Director, subject to the direction of the Board, shall be responsible to the Board and shall carry out the metric conversion program, pursuant to the provisions of this subchapter and the policies established by the Board.

children.heading: Executive Director; salary
children.label: b Executive Director; salary
children.text: The Executive Director of the Board shall serve full time and be subject to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5. The annual salary of the Executive Director shall not exceed level III of the Executive Schedule under section 5314 of such title.

children.heading: Staff personnel; appointment and compensation
children.label: c Staff personnel; appointment and compensation
children.text: The Board may appoint and fix the compensation of such staff personnel as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this subchapter in accordance with the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5.

children.heading: Experts and consultants; employment and compensation; annual review of contracts
children.label: d Experts and consultants; employment and compensation; annual review of contracts
children.text: The Board may (1) employ experts and consultants or organizations thereof, as authorized by ; (2) compensate individuals so employed at rates not in excess of the rate currently being paid grade 18 of the General Schedule under section 5332 of such title, including traveltime; and (3) may allow such individuals, while away from their homes or regular places of business, travel expenses (including per diem in lieu of subsistence) as authorized by section 5703 of such title 5 for persons in the Government service employed intermittently: , That contracts for such temporary employment may be renewed annually.section 3109 of title 5Provided, however
